volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 1571 - Section 1620)

sections.label: Section 1571
sections.original: λαβοῦσα τόνδε παῖδα Κεκροπίαν χθόνα
sections.translation: Do thou , Creusa , take this stripling and to Cecrops ’ land set forth ; and there upon the monarch ’ s throne establish him , for from Erechtheus ’ stock is he sprung , and therefore hath a right to rule that land of mine .

sections.label: Section 1575
sections.original: ἔσται τʼ ἀνʼ Ἑλλάδʼ εὐκλεής . οἱ τοῦδε γὰρ
sections.translation: Through Hellas shall his fame extend ; for his children ,— four branches springing from one root ,— shall give their names to the land and to the tribes of folk therein that dwell upon the rock I love . Teleona shall be the first ; and next in order shall come

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.original: Ὅπλητες Ἀργαδῆς τʼ , ἐμῆς τʼ ἀπʼ αἰγίδος
sections.translation: the Hopletes and Argades ; and then the Aegicores , called after my aegis , shall form one tribe . And their children again shall in the time appointed found an island home amid the Cyclades and on the sea-coast , thereby strengthening my country ;

sections.label: Section 1585
sections.original: δίδωσιν · ἀντίπορθμα δʼ ἠπείροιν δυοῖν
sections.translation: for they shall dwell upon the shores of two continents , of Europe and of Asia , on either side the strait ; and in honour of Ion ’ s name shall they be called Ionians and win them high renown . From Xuthus too and thee I see a common stock arise ;

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: Δῶρος μέν , ἔνθεν Δωρὶς ὑμνηθήσεται
sections.translation: Dorus , whence the famous Dorian state will spring ; and after him Achaeus in the land of Pelops ; he shall lord it o ’ er the seaboard nigh to Rhium , and his folk , that bear his name , shall win the proud distinction of their leader ’ s title .

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: καλῶς δʼ Ἀπόλλων πάντʼ ἔπραξε · πρῶτα μὲν
sections.translation: Thus in all hath Apollo rightly done ; first did he deliver thee of thy babe without sickness , so that thy friends knew naught ; and after thou didst bear this child and in swaddling-clothes hadst laid him , he bade Hermes carry him in his arms hither ,

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.original: ἔθρεψέ τʼ οὐδʼ εἴασεν ἐκπνεῦσαι βίον .
sections.translation: and did rear him , suffering him not to die . Now therefore hold thy peace as to this thy child ’ s real parentage , that Xuthus may delight in his fond fancy , and thou , lady , continue to enjoy thy blessing . So fare ye well ! for to you I

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: εὐδαίμονʼ ὑμῖν πότμον ἐξαγγέλλομαι .
sections.translation: bring tidings of a happier fate after this respite from affliction .

sections.label: Section 1606
sections.original: ὦ Διὸς Παλλὰς μεγίστου θύγατερ , οὐκ ἀπιστίᾳ
sections.translation: O Pallas , daughter of almighty Zeus , in full assurance will we accept thy words ; for I am convinced of my parentage from Loxias and this lady ; which even before was not incredible .

sections.label: Section 1609
sections.original: τἀμὰ νῦν ἄκουσον · αἰνῶ Φοῖβον οὐκ αἰνοῦσα πρίν ,
sections.translation: To what I say give ear . My former blame of Phoebus now is turned to praise ,

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.original: οὕνεχʼ οὗ ποτʼ ἠμέλησε παιδὸς ἀποδίδωσί μοι .
sections.translation: because he now restores to me the babe whom erst he slighted . Now are these portals fair unto mine eyes and this oracle of the god , though before I hated them . With joy now I even cling to the knocker on the door and salute the gates .

sections.label: Section 1614
sections.original: ᾔνεσʼ οὕνεκʼ εὐλογεῖς θεὸν μεταβαλοῦσʼ · ἀεὶ γὰρ οὖν
sections.translation: I commend thee for thy sudden change , and thy fair words about the god .

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.original: χρόνια μὲν τὰ τῶν θεῶν πως , ἐς τέλος δʼ οὐκ ἀσθενῆ .
sections.translation: ’ Tis ever thus ; Heaven ’ s justice may tarry awhile , yet comes it at the last in no wise weakened .

sections.label: Section 1616
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , στείχωμεν οἴκους .
sections.translation: My son , let us set out for home .

sections.label: Section 1616b
sections.original: στείχεθʼ , ἕψομαι δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Go ; I will follow .

sections.label: Section 1617
sections.original: ἀξία γʼ ἡμῶν ὁδουρός .
sections.translation: A guide we well may prize .

sections.label: Section 1617b
sections.original: καὶ φιλοῦσά γε πτόλιν .
sections.translation: Aye , and one that holds our city dear .

sections.label: Section 1618
sections.original: ἐς θρόνους δʼ ἵζου παλαιούς .
sections.translation: Go , sit thee down upon the throne of thy ancestors .

sections.label: Section 1618b
sections.original: ἄξιον τὸ κτῆμά μοι .
sections.translation: ’ Tis my heritage and I value it .

sections.label: Section 1619
sections.original: ὦ Διὸς Λητοῦς τʼ Ἄπολλον , χαῖρʼ · ὅτῳ δʼ ἐλαύνεται
sections.translation: All hail , Apollo , son of Zeus and Latona ! ’ Tis only right that he ,

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.original: συμφοραῖς οἶκος , σέβοντα δαίμονας θαρσεῖν χρεών ·
sections.translation: whose house is sore beset with trouble , should reverence God and keep good heart ; for at the last the righteous find their just reward , but the wicked , as their nature is , will never prosper .
